3.1  Online A/B Test
3.1.1 Workflow Specification
We conduct an online A/B test on a real-world
industrial video recommendation platform to
evaluate the effectiveness of TagCF-ut.  The
platform serves videos for over half a billion
users daily, and the item pool contains tens of
millions of videos. Figure 3 provides a detailed
overview of the implementation of our online
recommendation workflow. The tag extraction
module, the collaborative logic reasoning mod-
ule, and the training of all augmented models
are offline procedures executed on a daily basis.
In contrast, the inference part of the tag-logic in-
tegration module is deployed in the last ranking
stage (that chooses top-6 scored items as recom-
mendation from 120 candidates from previous
stage) for real-time recommendation requests,
with preprocessed tag and logic information re-
trieved from the latest knowledge base. As we
have described in Section 2.1 and Section 2.2,
we use the cover set solution to achieve stable
F
and efficient inference that fulfills the industrial
r
demand.
